questions.title: Q1. Fundamental Quantity
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Mass is one of the 7 fundamental quantities in SI system. Force, momentum, and density can be expressed in terms of fundamental quantities.

---

questions.title: Q2. Dimensional Formula
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Work = Force × Distance = (MLT⁻²) × (L) = ML²T⁻². This is also the dimensional formula for energy.

---

questions.title: Q3. Unit Conversion
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Kelvin is the SI base unit for temperature. Celsius is commonly used but is not a base unit.

---

questions.title: Q4. Significant Figures
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Leading zeros are not significant. The significant digits are 4, 5, and the trailing zero.

---

questions.title: Q5. Error Analysis
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Relative error = Absolute error/Measured value = 1/10 = 0.1. This is also 10% when expressed as percentage.

---

questions.title: Q6. Vernier Caliper
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
LC = Value of one main scale division / Number of vernier divisions = 1 mm / 50 = 0.02 mm

---

questions.title: Q7. Dimensional Consistency
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
v and u have dimensions LT⁻¹. at has dimensions (LT⁻²)(T) = LT⁻¹. So all terms match.

---

questions.title: Q8. Prefix Conversion
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
micro (μ) = 10⁻⁶, so 1 μg = 10⁻⁶ g

---

questions.title: Q9. Percentage Error
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Percentage error = (absolute error / measured value) × 100 = (1/50) × 100 = 2%

---

questions.title: Q10. Significant Figures
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
In multiplication, result has same significant figures as the number with fewest significant figures (2.5 has 2 sf).

---

questions.title: Q11. Derived Quantity
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Acceleration = velocity/time = (LT⁻¹)/T = LT⁻², derived from length and time.

---

questions.title: Q12. Zero Error
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Positive zero error occurs when the vernier scale's zero mark is to the right of the main scale's zero mark.

---

questions.title: Q13. Error in Sum
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
For addition, absolute errors add: Δ(a+b) = 0.1 + 0.1 = 0.2

---

questions.title: Q15. Dimensions
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Pressure = Force/Area = (MLT⁻²)/L² = ML⁻¹T⁻²

---

questions.title: Q16. Order of Magnitude
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Order of magnitude is the power of 10 when rounded to nearest integer. 5.6 rounds to 10, giving 10⁵.

---

questions.title: Q18. Least Count
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
LC = Pitch / Number of divisions = 0.5 mm / 100 = 0.005 mm = 5 μm

---

questions.title: Q19. Significant Figures
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Scientific notation clearly indicates significant figures. 1.0 × 10³ has 2 significant figures.

---

questions.title: Q20. Dimensional Formula
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
From F = Gm₁m₂/r², G = Fr²/m₁m₂ = (MLT⁻²)(L²)/(M²) = M⁻¹L³T⁻²

---

questions.title: Q21. Units
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Candela (cd) is one of the 7 SI base units for luminous intensity.

---

questions.title: Q22. Systematic Error
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Systematic errors are consistent and can be corrected by proper calibration of instruments.

---

questions.title: Q23. Random Error
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Random errors fluctuate and can be reduced by averaging multiple readings.

---

questions.title: Q24. Addition of Errors
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
For both addition and subtraction, absolute errors add: Δ(x-y) = Δx + Δy

---

questions.title: Q25. Dimensions
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Momentum has dimensions MLT⁻¹, while force, impulse, and thrust all have MLT⁻².

---

questions.title: Q26. Prefixes
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
nano = 10⁻⁹, so 1 nm = 10⁻⁹ m

---

questions.title: Q27. Trigonometry
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
sin 90° = 1 (perpendicular = hypotenuse in a right triangle)

---

questions.title: Q28. Vernier Reading
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Reading = Main scale + Vernier × LC = 2.1 + 5 × 0.01 = 2.1 + 0.05 = 2.15 cm

---

questions.title: Q29. Error Percentage
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Percentage error = (± 2/100) × 100 = 2%

---

questions.title: Q30. Significant Figures
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
In addition, result should have same number of decimal places as least precise term (10.5 has 1 decimal).

---

questions.title: Q31. Fundamental Units
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
SI system has 7 base/fundamental units: m, kg, s, K, A, cd, mol

---

questions.title: Q32. Dimensionless
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Refractive index = speed of light in vacuum / speed in medium = ratio of same quantities = dimensionless

---

questions.title: Q33. Prefixes Order
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Tera (T) = 10¹² is the largest among these common prefixes.

---

questions.title: Q34. Error in Power
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Relative error in r³ = 3 × Δr/r = 3 × 0.1/5 = 0.06
r³ = 125 cm³, Error = 0.06 × 125 = 7.5 cm³

---

questions.title: Q35. Coherent System
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Coherent means derived units can be expressed without any conversion factor (e.g., 1 N = 1 kg × 1 m/s²)

---

questions.title: Q36. Accuracy vs Precision
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
The readings are close to each other (high precision), but we don't know the true value to determine accuracy.

---

questions.title: Q37. Dimensional Analysis Use
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Dimensional analysis can check equation correctness and derive form, but cannot determine dimensionless constants (like 1/2 in KE).

---

questions.title: Q38. Vernier Formula
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
LC = Value of one main scale division / Number of vernier divisions

---

questions.title: Q39. Rounding
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
3.456 → look at 4th digit (6 > 5), round up → 3.46

---

questions.title: Q40. Time Units
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
1 hour = 60 minutes = 60 × 60 = 3600 seconds

---

questions.title: Q41. Sine Rule
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
sin(180° - θ) = sin θ (property of sine function)

---

questions.title: Q42. Error Addition
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Δ(x-y) = Δx + Δy = 1 + 2 = 3, so result = 5 ± 3

---

questions.title: Q43. Kilogram Definition
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
In 2019, kilogram was redefined using Planck's constant (h = 6.62607015 × 10⁻³⁴ kg·m²/s)

---

questions.title: Q44. Scre w Gauge Error
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Positive zero error when reading is positive (to right of zero). Here 3 divisions × 0.01 mm = 0.03 mm

---

questions.title: Q45. Logarithm
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
log₁₀(10⁵) = 5 (because 10⁵ = 10 raised to power 5)

---

questions.title: Q47. Exponential
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Any number raised to power 0 equals 1 (except 0⁰ which is undefined)

---

questions.title: Q48. Pressure Unit
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Pascal (Pa) = N/m² is the SI derived unit for pressure

---

questions.title: Q49. Error Multiplication
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
For multiplication and division, relative errors add: Δz/z = Δx/x + Δy/y

---

questions.title: Q50. Decimal Places
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Least decimal places = 1 (in 12.5), so result should have 1 decimal: 14.2? Wait - 12.5 + 1.23 = 13.73, + 0.456 = 14.186 → 14.19

Actually: least is 1 decimal (12.5), so result to 1 decimal: 14.2

### Answer: C (corrected)

---

questions.title: Q51. Momentum Dimensions
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Momentum = mass × velocity = M × LT⁻¹ = MLT⁻¹

---

questions.title: Q52. Prefixes Power
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
pico = 10⁻¹²

---

questions.title: Q53. Random Error Formula
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Random error = (Maximum value - Minimum value) / Number of readings

---

questions.title: Q54. Measurement Types
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Using a scale to measure length directly compares with standard (the scale markings)

---

questions.title: Q55. Dimensional Equation
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
v² = L²T⁻², 2gh = L × LT⁻² = L²T⁻². Dimensionally consistent.

---

questions.title: Q56. SI Unit of Current
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Ampere (A) is the SI base unit for electric current.

---

questions.title: Q57. Least Count Choice
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Screw gauge has LC = 0.01 mm = 10 μm, more precise than vernier caliper (0.1 mm)

---

questions.title: Q60. Exponential Form
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
7 zeros after decimal: 10⁻⁷

---

questions.title: Q61. Error in Sum Same Units
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
For series addition: ΔR_total = 1 + 1 = 2 Ω

---

questions.title: Q62. Force Dimensions
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Force = mass × acceleration = M × LT⁻² = MLT⁻²

---

questions.title: Q64. Decimal to Binary
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
log(1) = 0 because 10⁰ = 1

---

questions.title: Q67. Parallax
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Parallax occurs when you view the scale at an angle. Viewing perpendicularly eliminates this error.

---

questions.title: Q68. Error Propagation
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
If x → ax, then Δ(ax) = aΔx. Here 2 × 0.5 = 1.0

---

questions.title: Q69. Derived Unit
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Force = mass × acceleration = kg × (m/s²) = kg·m/s², called Newton (N)

---

questions.title: Q70. Percentage Error
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Percentage error = (0.5/25) × 100 = 2%

---

questions.title: Q71. Significant: Decimal
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Leading zeros don't count; trailing zero in decimal counts. So 0.0100 = 1, 0, 0 = 3 significant figures.

---

questions.title: Q72. Error Analysis
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Random errors fluctuate above and below the true value, sometimes positive, sometimes negative.

---

questions.title: Q73. Dimensional Check
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
√(l/g) = √(L/LT⁻²) = √(T²) = T, so dimensions match (time)

---

questions.title: Q74. Trigonometry
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
cos 0° = 1 (base = hypotenuse in right triangle)

---

questions.title: Q75. Least Count: Screw
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
LC = Pitch / number of divisions = 1 mm / 100 = 0.01 mm

---

questions.title: Q76. Multiplication: Sig Fig
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
8.0 has 2 sf, 2.5 has 2 sf → result has 2 sf: 20 (or 2.0 × 10¹)

---

questions.title: Q77. Error Analysis
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Systematic errors consistently shift measurements in one direction (always high or always low).

---

questions.title: Q78. Units: Force
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Newton (N) = kg × m/s² in SI base units

---

questions.title: Q79. Prefixes: Large
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Tera (T) = 10¹², so 1 Tm = 10¹² m

---

questions.title: Q80. Square Root Error
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Relative error in √r = (1/2) × (0.3/9) = 0.0167, √r = 3 cm, error = 0.05 cm

---

questions.title: Q81. Logarithm: e
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
ln(1) = 0 because e⁰ = 1

---

questions.title: Q82. SI Base Units
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Mole (mol) is the SI base unit for amount of substance

---

questions.title: Q83. Precision vs Accuracy
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Repeated similar readings show high precision (reproducibility), not necessarily accuracy.

---

questions.title: Q84. Derivative Integration
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
d/dx(x³) = 3x² (power rule: bring down power, reduce power by 1)

---

questions.title: Q85. Error in Constant
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Multiplying by exact constant doesn't change relative error. Absolute error scales with the constant.

---

questions.title: Q86. Dimension Check
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
√(g/L) gives dimensions of time, matching time period T. 2π is dimensionless.

---

questions.title: Q87. Units: Energy
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Joule = Newton × meter = (kg·m/s²) × m = kg·m²/s². Also equals 1 N·m.

---

questions.title: Q88. Error: Subtraction
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
For subtraction, absolute errors add: Δ(x-y) = 0.2 + 0.1 = 0.3

---

questions.title: Q90. Trigonometry Value
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
tan 45° = 1 because opposite = adjacent in isosceles right triangle

---

questions.title: Q91. Error Percent
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Percentage error = (1/25) × 100% = 4%

---

questions.title: Q92. Prefixes: Small
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
femto (f) = 10⁻¹⁵

---

questions.title: Q93. Error Propagation
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
For addition and subtraction, absolute uncertainties always add up

---

questions.title: Q94. Dimensional Formula
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
From PV = nRT, R = PV/(nT) = (ML⁻¹T⁻² × L³)/(N × Θ) = ML²T⁻²Θ⁻¹N⁻¹

---

questions.title: Q95. Units: Pressure
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Pa = N/m² = kg/(m·s²)

---

questions.title: Q96. Significant: Integer
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
Without decimal point, trailing zeros are not significant. Write 5000. or 5.0 × 10³ for more sf.

---

questions.title: Q97. Error Analysis
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
For single reading, error = ±(Least count)/2 = ±0.5 mm for 1 mm LC

---

questions.title: Q98. Derivative: Log
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
d/dx(ln x) = 1/x (natural log derivative)

---

questions.title: Q99. Integration
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
∫x² dx = x³/3 + C (power rule: increase power by 1, divide by new power)

---

questions.title: Q100. Error: Addition
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation:
For addition, absolute errors add up: Δ(x+y) = Δx + Δy

---

questions.title: Q1. Fundamental Quantity Identification
questions.subject: Physics
questions.given: Which of the following is NOT a fundamental quantity?
questions.answer:
✅ **B) Force**

### Explanation:
Force is a derived quantity (MLT⁻²). Length, time, and temperature are fundamental quantities.
questions.explanation:
Force is a derived quantity (MLT⁻²). Length, time, and temperature are fundamental quantities.

---

questions.title: Q2. Dimensional Formula
questions.subject: Physics
questions.given: Find the dimensional formula of pressure.
questions.find: Pressure in terms of M, L, T

questions.title: Q3. Unit Conversion
questions.subject: Physics
questions.given: Convert 5 km to meters.

questions.title: Q4. Significant Figures
questions.subject: Physics
questions.given: How many significant figures are in 0.00520?
questions.solution:
Leading zeros don't count, trailing zero in decimal counts.
0.00520 → 3 significant figures

questions.title: Q5. Error Calculation
questions.subject: Physics
questions.given: Measured value = 10.5 cm, Absolute error = 0.1 cm. Find relative error.

questions.title: Q6. Least Count
questions.subject: Physics
questions.given: A vernier scale has 20 divisions over 19 mm. Find the least count.

questions.title: Q7. Prefix Conversion
questions.subject: Physics
questions.given: Convert 2.5 μA to ampere.

questions.title: Q8. Dimensional Check
questions.subject: Physics
questions.given: Check if v = u + at is dimensionally correct.
questions.solution:
- v: LT⁻¹
- u: LT⁻¹
- at: (LT⁻²)(T) = LT⁻¹
All have same dimensions → Dimensionally correct

questions.title: Q9. Order of Magnitude
questions.subject: Physics

questions.solution:

questions.given:
Which is a derived quantity?
A) Mass
B) Time
C) Density
D) Temperature
questions.answer:
✅ **C) Density**

### Explanation:
Density = Mass/Volume = ML⁻³ (derived from mass and length)
questions.explanation:
Density = Mass/Volume = ML⁻³ (derived from mass and length)

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is electric charge?
flashcards.answer: Electric charge is a fundamental property of matter that causes it to experience electromagnetic forces. It comes in two types: positive and negative. Like charges repel, unlike charges attract.
flashcards.memoryTrick:
[!TIP]
"P.E.N." — Positive means Electron deficiency, Negative means Electron excess!

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: State Coulomb's law
flashcards.answer: The force between two point charges is directly proportional to the product of their charges and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Define electric field
flashcards.answer: Electric field at a point is the force experienced by a unit positive test charge placed at that point, assuming the test charge doesn't disturb the source charge distribution.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Electric field due to a point charge Q at distance r?

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: How do you find total electric field from multiple charges?
flashcards.answer: Electric field is a vector quantity. Total field is the vector sum of individual fields from all charges.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: State Gauss's Law
flashcards.answer: The total electric flux through a closed surface is equal to 1/ε₀ times the total charge enclosed by the surface.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is dipole moment of an electric dipole?
flashcards.answer: Dipole moment is the product of charge and separation, directed from negative to positive charge.
flashcards.memoryTrick:
p = q × d, direction from -q to +q!

---

flashcards.question: How are force and electric field related?
flashcards.answer:
Force = charge × field: F = qE
Field = force/charge: E = F/q

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Value of ε₀?

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is charge quantization?
flashcards.answer: Charge exists only in discrete integer multiples of elementary charge e = 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ C

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Conservation of charge
flashcards.answer: Total charge in an isolated system remains constant. Charge can neither be created nor destroyed.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Additivity of charge
flashcards.answer: Total charge is the algebraic sum of individual charges.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Force superposition principle
flashcards.answer: Net force on any charge equals vector sum of forces due to all other charges.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Properties of electric field lines
flashcards.answer:
1. Start from positive, end at negative
2. Never form closed loops
3. Never intersect
4. Density indicates field strength
5. Tangent gives field direction

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: How does field line density indicate field strength?

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Properties of ideal test charge
flashcards.answer:
1. Infinitesimally small (doesn't disturb field)
2. Positive (by convention)
3. Point charge

Why Small?
[!IMPORTANT]
So it doesn't alter the source charge distribution!

flashcards.question: What is a Gaussian surface?
flashcards.answer:
An imaginary closed surface used to calculate electric flux conveniently.

Ideal for Gauss's Law:
- Spherical (point charge, sphere)
- Cylindrical (line charge)
- Box (sheet charge)

flashcards.question: Electric field inside uniformly charged solid sphere (r < R)?

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Electric potential inside uniformly charged sphere?
flashcards.formula:
$$V = \frac{kQ}{2R^3}(3R^2 - r^2)$$

At Center (r = 0):
$$V_{center} = \frac{3kQ}{2R}$$

At Surface (r = R):
$$V_{surface} = \frac{kQ}{R}$$

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Why is field between plates (σ/ε₀) double the field from single sheet (σ/2ε₀)?
flashcards.answer:
Field from each sheet adds up (same direction)
Two sheets: E = σ/2ε₀ + σ/2ε₀ = σ/ε₀
Single sheet: E = σ/2ε₀ (points both ways!)

flashcards.answer: Molecules with permanent dipole moment (e.g., H₂O, HCl).

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What are non-polar molecules?
flashcards.answer: Molecules with no permanent dipole moment (e.g., N₂, O₂, CH₄).

At edges, field lines bulge (fringing).

| Constant at 0 | Increases linearly |

flashcards.answer:
1. Lines emerge from both charges

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Derive energy density formula
flashcards.answer:
Start with U = QV/2 for capacitor
V = Ed, Q = σA, C = ε₀A/d

$$U = \frac{1}{2}CV^2 = \frac{1}{2}\varepsilon_0 A \frac{E^2 d^2}{d^2} = \frac{1}{2}\varepsilon_0 E^2(Ad)$$

flashcards.question: What is the difference between fundamental and derived quantities?
flashcards.answer:
Fundamental quantities are independent and cannot be expressed in terms of other quantities (7 in SI). Derived quantities can be expressed in terms of fundamental quantities (e.g., speed = length/time).

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Name all 7 SI base units.
flashcards.answer:
Meter (m) - Length, Kilogram (kg) - Mass, Second (s) - Time, Kelvin (K) - Temperature, Ampere (A) - Current, Candela (cd) - Luminous intensity, Mole (mol) - Amount of substance.

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is dimensional formula?
flashcards.answer:
Expression showing how a physical quantity depends on fundamental quantities. Example: [Force] = MLT⁻²

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Define least count.
flashcards.answer:
Smallest value that can be measured accurately by an instrument. LC = value of one main scale division / number of vernier/scale divisions.

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What are the rules for significant figures?
flashcards.answer:
1. All non-zero digits are significant. 2. Zeros between non-zero digits are significant. 3. Leading zeros are never significant. 4. Trailing zeros in decimal are significant.

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Differentiate between systematic and random errors.
flashcards.answer:
Systematic errors are consistent (always high or always low), can be eliminated by calibration. Random errors fluctuate above/below true value, reduced by averaging.

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: How to calculate absolute error?
flashcards.answer:
For single measurement: LC/2. For multiple measurements: (max - min)/2

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is relative error?
flashcards.answer:
Ratio of absolute error to measured value. Relative error = Δx/x (dimensionless). Percentage error = (Δx/x) × 100%

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: How do errors propagate in addition?
flashcards.answer:
For z = x ± y, Δz = Δx + Δy (absolute errors add)

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: How do errors propagate in multiplication?
flashcards.answer:
For z = xy or x/y, relative errors add: Δz/z = Δx/x + Δy/y

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: If z = xⁿ, what is error propagation?
flashcards.answer:
Relative error multiplies by n: Δz/z = |n| × (Δx/x)

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is the least count of a vernier caliper?
flashcards.answer:
LC = (One main scale division) / (Number of vernier divisions). Usually 0.01 cm or 0.1 mm.

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Least count of screw gauge formula?
flashcards.answer:
LC = Pitch / Number of circular scale divisions. Common: 0.01 mm

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is positive zero error in vernier caliper?
flashcards.answer:
When vernier zero is to the right of main scale zero. Subtract the error from reading.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is dimensional homogeneity?
flashcards.answer:
All terms in a physical equation have same dimensions. Example: In v = u + at, all terms have LT⁻¹

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is a dimensionless quantity?
flashcards.answer:
A quantity with all zero exponents in dimensional formula. Example: refractive index, angle in radians.

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Differentiate accuracy and precision.
flashcards.answer:
Accuracy: closeness to true value. Precision: closeness of measurements to each other.

flashcards.question: How to correct positive zero error?

---

flashcards.question: How to correct negative zero error?

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: How to reduce random error?

---

flashcards.question: How to eliminate systematic error?

Tags: Flashcards UnitsAndMeasurements Physics JEE Class11

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Name the seven fundamental SI quantities and their units.
flashcards.answer:
1. Mass - kilogram (kg)
2. Length - metre (m)
3. Time - second (s)
4. Electric current - ampere (A)
5. Temperature - kelvin (K)
6. Amount of substance - mole (mol)
7. Luminous intensity - candela (cd)
flashcards.memoryTrick:
"Ma Li Ti Am Ke Mo Ca" (First letters)

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Convert km/h to m/s and vice versa.
flashcards.answer:
km/h → m/s: divide by 3.6
m/s → km/h: multiply by 3.6
flashcards.memoryTrick:
"3-6-0-0" - 3600 seconds in hour, 1000 meters in km

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Formula for least count of vernier calipers.
flashcards.answer:
LC = (1 MSD) / n
where n = number of VSD divisions

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Formula for least count of screw gauge.
flashcards.answer: LC = Pitch / Number of divisions on thimble

flashcards.question: Dimensional formula of pressure.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Are leading zeros significant?
flashcards.answer: NO - Leading zeros (zeros to the left of first non-zero digit) are NEVER significant.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Are trailing zeros significant?
flashcards.answer:
It depends:
- With decimal point: YES (2.500 has 4 SF)
- Without decimal point: AMBIGUOUS (1500 could be 1, 2, or 4 SF)

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: If Z = A + B, what is ΔZ?

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: If Z = A - B, what is ΔZ?
flashcards.answer: ΔZ = ΔA + ΔB (errors ADD even for subtraction!)
flashcards.memoryTrick:
"SUMDIFF = ADD" - Sum OR Difference, errors ADD

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: If Z = A × B, what is ΔZ/Z?
flashcards.answer: ΔZ/Z = ΔA/A + ΔB/B (Relative errors ADD in multiplication)

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: If Z = A/B, what is ΔZ/Z?
flashcards.answer:
ΔZ/Z = ΔA/A + ΔB/B (Relative errors ADD in division)

Same as multiplication!

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: If Z = Aⁿ, what is ΔZ/Z?
flashcards.answer: ΔZ/Z = |n| × ΔA/A (Relative error multiplied by exponent)

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is positive zero error and how to correct?
flashcards.answer: When VSD zero is to the RIGHT of MSD zero → reading is too HIGH

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is negative zero error and how to correct?
flashcards.answer: When VSD zero is to the LEFT of MSD zero → reading is too LOW

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What is principle of dimensional homogeneity?
flashcards.answer: In any physically meaningful equation, all terms must have the same dimensions.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What CANNOT be determined by dimensional analysis?
flashcards.answer:
1. Value of dimensionless constants (π, e, 2, etc.)
2. Distinction between quantities with same dimensions (torque vs energy)
flashcards.memoryTrick:
"Dimless Constants Are Invisible" to dimensional analysis

---

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Formula for relative error.

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: Convert g/cm³ to kg/m³ and vice versa.
flashcards.answer:
g/cm³ → kg/m³: multiply by 1000
kg/m³ → g/cm³: divide by 1000

flashcards.subject: Physics
flashcards.question: What are the 5 rules for counting significant figures?
flashcards.answer:
1. Non-zero digits are always significant
2. Zeros between non-zero digits are significant
3. Leading zeros are never significant
4. Trailing zeros after decimal ARE significant
5. Trailing zeros without decimal are AMBIGUOUS

flashcards.answer: Exact numbers (counted, defined, or from formulas like 2π) have INFINITE significant figures and don't limit the precision.

3. Find dimensions of constants
4. Convert between unit systems
